show.title: Tiny Furniture
show.headline: Lena Dunham explores an authentic portrait of a young woman at a crossroads

event.title: <show-title>Tiny Furniture</show-title>
event.headline: Lena Dunham’s awkwardly charming debut reflects her longstanding career
event.description:
Although a polarizing figure, Lena Dunham has created some of the most quintessential millennial coming-of-age films of the 2010s. Her breakout, oddball SXSW indie TINY FURNITURE was the blueprint for her hit TV series Girls, and also starred Jemima Kirke and Alex Karpovsky.

In the film, Dunham plays a version of herself: Aura, an aspiring film graduate who returns to live with her mom and sister in New York after feeling the independence of college. Aura is ready for her career to begin, but doesn’t know where to begin, and takes up a restaurant gig to make money while she sorts out her goals.

Dunham’s projects have always felt personal, but there’s a certain rawness to TINY FURNITURE that’s uncomfortably disturbing but almost impossible to look away from the trainwreck. In the wake of her new memoir Famesick, it’s nostalgic to go back to see where it all began.

show.title: Orlando
show.headline: A journey through love and discovery

event.title: <show-title>Orlando</show-title>
event.headline: Explore the fluidity of gender and time with immortal fae creature Tilda Swinton
event.description:
"Same person. No difference at all..."

Let’s talk about gender, y’all! The complex and sticky social construct of behavioral traits and visual cues that, for some reason, tells us how to treat other people and what color explosives to deploy upon its reveal. Yeah, gender, an easy subject to unpack for sure. Wait, gender doesn’t exist in a vacuum, right? (Does anything exist in a vacuum?) I digress. Gender and the fluidity of identity are at the heart of this month’s Queer Film Theory 101 screening. ORLANDO, adapted in 1992 by filmmaker Sally Potter from the 1920s novel by Virginia Woolf, is perhaps the holy grail of gender-bending historical fantasy, and yet, 30+ years later, not enough people have seen it!

An unconventional epic narrative with frequent fourth-wall breaks, ORLANDO follows the supernaturally long life of its title character (Tilda Swinton) as he is granted immortality by Queen Elizabeth I (Quentin Crisp). Beginning in the 1600s and ending in the early '90s, ORLANDO explores the various historical events, relationships, trials, and achievements of its boundaryless lead. Think INTERVIEW WITH THE VAMPIRE, but with fewer vampires and more Billy Zane. Gorgeous, sumptuously decadent, and deeply subversive, this film is a must-see for any queer film lover. The striking visual style and modernist approach to period drama in ORLANDO became massively influential across film, prestige television (*cough* Fleabag), and fashion; in particular, the costumes of prolific designer Sandy Powell are a feast for the eyes. See you in time. Wait…is time a construct too?

show.title: American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez
show.headline: A portrait of the founder of modern Chicano theatre and film

landscapeHeroImage.altText: AMERICAN PACHUCO: THE LEGEND OF LUIS VALDEZ

portraitHeroImage.altText: AMERICAN PACHUCO: THE LEGEND OF LUIS VALDEZ

posterImages.altText: AMERICAN PACHUCO: THE LEGEND OF LUIS VALDEZ

event.title: <show-title>American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Live Q&A with the filmmaker and documentary subject
event.description:
In-person Q&A with filmmaker David Alvarado and documentary subject Luis Valdez following the screening. On 7/24, they will be joined by actor Lou Diamond Phillips.

An auteur emerges from America’s underclass: from migrant farmworker to revolutionary artist, Luis Valdez changed American culture. In the 1960s, his El Teatro Campesino performed on flatbed trucks and helped mobilize workers to win the first farmworker union contract. His Zoot Suit was the first Chicano play on Broadway. Despite critical rejection that killed the show, he persevered, creating LA BAMBA – a breakout blockbuster that authentically depicted Mexican-American life to the world. Now in its 60th year, El Teatro continues to be a beacon for Latino/a creators. This is the story of an artist who gave voice to the overlooked and opened pathways for generations.

portraitHeroImage.altText: AMERICAN PACHUCO: THE LEGEND OF LUIS VALDEZ

show.title: The Phantom of the Opera (2004)
show.headline: The classic musical comes to life on the big screen

event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>The Phantom of the Opera (2004)</show-title>
event.headline: Craft in the shadows of a classic romance
event.description: How’s this for a strange duet? Joel Schumacher’s 2004 gothic romance paired with hands-on crafting designed just for the movie. Each guest receives two craft sets to work on from their seat, including Phantom-inspired calligraphy with guided activity sheets and a card to fill in, sign, and seal. You’ll also create an iconic red paper rose with step-by-step instructions, all while enjoying food and drinks delivered right to your seat.

show.title: Friday Night Lights (TV)
show.headline: In a small rural town, the state football championship is prized above all else

event.title: <show-title>Friday Night Lights (TV)</show-title> <event-type-title>20th Anniversary Roadshow w/Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline:
Presented by ATX TV and 
      Plex
event.description:
Join us for a 20th Anniversary celebration of the beloved NBC series Friday Night Lights, presented by Plex and ATX TV. Co-hosted by Adrianne Palicki (Tyra Collette), each event screens an episode handpicked by a featured cast member. Follow ATX TV on Plex to read their Plex Discussion on why that episode meant so much to them. Then hear it straight from the source with live commentary, behind-the-scenes stories, and audience Q&A. Reserve your seat with a refundable $5 food & beverage voucher redeemable at the screening.

Since a road trip to Dillon needs the right boots, we’ve teamed up with Tecovas to bring Texas style to every stop. They’re outfitting the cast on stage, but also raffling cowboy boots and hats to guests. Plus, all ticketholders can enjoy 10% off at their local Tecovas store for a limited time around the Roadshow. Stay tuned for more surprises along the way!

Photo rights: Todd McMullen

eventType.title: Free Victory Screening
eventType.headline: Our way of saying thank you to our Victory Members
eventType.description: Calling all Victory Members! We’re saying thanks with a free screening. Just buy a fully refundable food & beverage voucher to reserve your seat, and then use it toward food and drink at the show.

show.title: The Odyssey
show.headline: Christopher Nolan crafts an epic in every sense of the word

event.title: <show-title>The Odyssey</show-title> <event-type-title>Feast of the Gods Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: A movie feast for the gods
event.description:
Join us for the Feast of the Gods Screening, a one-night-only screening paired with a multi-course menu inspired by the flavors of the ancient Mediterranean. Enjoy house-made focaccia, pork belly skewers, lamb-and-beef albondigas, salmon en croute, and more, culminating in a decadent dessert of red wine-poached pears and dark chocolate. It's the feast that launched a thousand ships, all for the price of a standard movie ticket.

Please note that vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free modifications are not available for this offering.

show.title: Elf
show.headline: The best way to spread Christmas cheer

event.title: <show-title>Elf</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A cheerful celebration with themed games, props & singing loud for all to hear
event.description:
"Buddy the Elf, what's your favorite color?"

Don’t be a cotton-headed ninnymuggins this holiday season. Come out to Alamo Drafthouse and spread the cheer at this sugar-fueled, interactive holiday spectacular, packed with games, props, and, of course, singing loud for all to hear.

Bundle up for this celebration of one of the best holiday films ever made, period. Will Ferrell’s take on a North Pole elf trying to figure out Manhattan (“Watch out! The yellow ones don’t stop!”) just might be the funniest he’s ever been.

Our ELF Movie Party features all sorts of fun props, a gift "wrap off" before the film, and of course, your very own elf hat.

contentBlocks.paragraphs: Whether it’s maple syrup or another refreshing beverage, it’ll go down better in our exclusive ELF 16 oz. collector’s glass. Featuring the movie logo on one side and the phrase “Cotton-headed Ninny-muggins” on the other, this pint glass is available for a limited time on AlamoMart.com. Order now and proudly celebrate Christmas in July at the ELF Movie Party.

show.headline: Start your engines

event.title: <show-title>The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A NOS-fueled party for family
event.description: Join us for a high-octane movie party celebrating the 25th anniversary of the original FAST movie. Before the cars in Tokyo drifted, before they went to space, and before any Vin Diesel feuds, it was just a simple undercover cop story about stolen VCRs, street racing, and loyalty tested at 100 miles per hour. Strap in for this special screening with a live host, exclusive themed keepsakes like a bumper magnet, bottle opener, and a NOS keychain, as well as a theater full of family.

show.title: The Substance
show.headline: A gorgeously gross beauty regimen that will change your life

event.title: <show-title>The Substance</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A gorgeously gross beauty regimen that will change your life
event.description:
Oh, the things we do for beauty. Look, we’re not saying we wouldn’t want to look like Margaret Qualley, but Demi Moore’s over-the-hill aerobic star goes to some pretty extreme measures. Her use of THE SUBSTANCE may transform her into a “younger, more beautiful, more perfect” version of herself (via a gloriously horrific rebirth), but the more she uses it, the worse the side effects become. It becomes increasingly apparent that beauty is only skin deep as both of her lives spiral into a truly ugly mess.

THE SUBSTANCE is a wild, toe-curling ride from writer/director Coralie Fargeat that features some of the most nerve-shredding body horror sequences in recent years. And with it taking home the award for Best Screenplay at the 2024 Cannes Film Festival, there’s plenty of substance behind the camera as well. As fantastical as the beauty solution is, THE SUBSTANCE’s themes of aging, youth, and self-esteem are very much grounded in our obsession with physical beauty.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Activator pen – For a younger, more beautiful, more perfect version of yourself.
  • Termination glass – But sometimes perfection is overrated.
  • Body horror toy – There may not be chicken wings in here, but it's still oddly satisfying to play with.
  • "You Are One" sticker – Respect the balance.
  • Walk of Fame keychain – Sometimes your legacy gets so mixed up, they even spell your name wrong.
  • Monstro Elisasue mask – Nothing else matters so long as you are pretty on the outside.

event.title: <show-title>Six: The Musical Live!</show-title> <event-type-title>Sing-Along</event-type-title>
event.headline: Belt out the hits from the West End smash
event.description:
Get ready to lose your head at this special sing-along screening of SIX: THE MUSICAL LIVE! This is your chance to rule the room alongside the queens and sing along with every iconic number in a theater full of fellow Tudorphiles.

Please note that our usual No Talking/No Singing Showtunes policy is relaxed for this screening, but please be reasonable and save a little vocal power for the rest of the monarchy.

show.title: Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town

event.title: <show-title>Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town
event.description:
They promised to reunite 10 years later, at 9:30, because McKinley had something at 11 and he’d already moved it twice. Now it’s 25 years later, and Camp Firewood is back in session and on the big screen. Exclusively at Alamo, we’re pairing this special anniversary party screening with a themed menu that promises more than canned vegetables, a custom camp T-shirt, and other surprises. Whether you’ve spent the last 25 years wondering if you taste like burger or just trying to make it to the campfire before somebody breaks into song, this is your chance to come back to Camp Firewood. We want you inside us.

All of our campers must adhere to a dress code, so each and every ticket includes a limited edition WET HOT AMERICAN SUMMER 25th Anniversary T-Shirt.

show.title: Mystery Voyage
show.headline: A 1990s kung fu fantasia from Hong Kong

event.title: <show-title>Mystery Voyage</show-title> <event-type-title>July 2026</event-type-title>
event.headline: An unhinged extraterrestrial space adventure
event.description: Running off the fumes from ALIENS, this unhinged extraterrestrial space adventure – written by the "American Tolkien" – has been preserved from an ultra-rare 35mm print.

show.title: The Conjuring 2
show.headline: Top-shelf poltergeist cinema mania!

event.title: <show-title>The Conjuring 2</show-title>
event.headline: Top-shelf poltergeist cinema mania!
event.description: Time to get serious about celebrating James Wan’s contributions to the world of sequels. This dude’s deep in with the expansive SAW, INSIDIOUS, FAST & FURIOUS, AQUAMAN and CONJURING universes, and he’s only been in the biz for 20 years. For THE CONJURING 2, Wan moves the Warrens’ ghost story cinemaquake to overseas where the upset spirits of jolly old England are dialed up to 11! Aside from the top-shelf jump scares, what really sets THE CONJURING 2 apart is the genuine stirred emotion for the terrified family.

show.title: Buffet Infinity
show.headline: A found footage cosmic gumbo

event.title: <show-title>Buffet Infinity</show-title> <event-type-title>+ MAN EATING P***Y Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: A double feature of oddball comedies
event.description:
About BUFFET INFINITY
2026 / 99 mins.

Echoing the Canadian comedy classic SCTV and picking from hundreds of hours of original, low-budget TV ads to tell the sinister tale of two restaurants battling it out in the fictional town of Westridge County. Ads for insurance, used car rivals, a local religious scholar, and a recording artist converge to tell the story of an expanding sinkhole, a cult, and an ever-growing restaurant that becomes unsettlingly sentient.

About MAN EATING P****Y
2026 / 15 mins

A terminally ill man finds solace with an enigmatic prostitute whose inhuman body provides both carnal satisfaction and final deliverance.

show.title: Einstürzende Neubauten: Liebeslieder
show.headline: An essential text for anyone with a love for the avant-garde

event.title: <show-title>Einstürzende Neubauten: Liebeslieder</show-title>
event.headline: An essential text for anyone with a love for the avant-garde
event.description:
"Destruction was, for us, never destruction in the sense of an act of violence. It was always about making room." – Blixa Bargeld

An essential text for anyone with a love for the avant-garde. The explosive soundscapes of industrial music titans Einstürzende Neubauten first shook Berlin, then the whole world to its core in the '80s. In LIEBESLIEDER, co-directors Klaus Maeck and Johanna Schenkel wield a fractured feast of killer Neubauten performances from Euro television: ones that highlight the group’s cinematic presence, wild handmade instruments, and totally unique style. If you’re at all curious about industrial or noise music in general, this sweaty documentary is unmissable. If you’re already a Neubauten fan, this humanizing portrait of their first decade will give you a fresh take on the band.

show.title: eXistenZ
show.headline: David Cronenberg's video game odyssey

event.title: <show-title>eXistenZ</show-title>
event.headline: Cronenberg's augmented reality video game head trip!
event.description: Lubricate your bioport and prepare to jack in to David Cronenberg's twisty thriller set in a near-future world of ultra-realistic video games, corporate espionage, and guns made from bones and teeth. Fleshy game systems connect directly to players' spinal columns via an umbilical-cord-like cable in Cronenberg's mutant vision of video game culture. Part Hitchcock's 39 STEPS, part VIDEODROME, EXISTENZ is pure Cronenberg paranoia, sex panic, and body horror. Add in a dash of existential crisis, and you have yourself one of the great sci-fi masterpieces of 1999.

show.title: Motor City
show.headline: Revenge, Detroit style

event.title: <show-title>Motor City</show-title> <event-type-title>Early Access</event-type-title>
event.headline: Be among the first to see this stylist throwback crime thriller
event.description:
Before the factories emptied out and the Motor City lost its shine, Detroit was all horsepower, soul, and swagger. MOTOR CITY drops you into that world, following a working-class romantic who gets framed by a ruthless gangster after falling for the wrong woman. Years later, he's out of prison with one thing on his mind: revenge.

Starring Alan Ritchson and Shailene Woodley, MOTOR CITY is a stylish throwback crime thriller fueled by muscle cars, sharp suits, and a killer soundtrack curated by Jack White. If you like your revenge stories lean, mean, and set to impeccable needle drops, this one's built to go fast.

show.title: Halloween II (1981)
show.headline: Debra Hill and John Carpenter’s iconic slasher returns.

event.title: <show-title>Halloween II (1981)</show-title>
event.headline: Michael Myers traps Jamie Lee Curtis in the horror hospital!
event.description:
"This movie has the first close-up I can remember of a hypodermic needle being inserted into an eyeball." - Roger Ebert.

Stunning splatter kills, Jamie Lee Curtis in hazy David Lynch mode, an increasingly unhinged Donald Pleasance as “Dr. Loomis” and a glossy reprise of John Carpenter’s iconic creepy HALLOWEEN theme are four major reasons to see this classic slasher sequel in the dark amongst friends. Starting up mere seconds after the events of Carpenter’s first HALLOWEEN, Loomis wanders the gardens, classrooms, alleys and morgues of Haddonfield in order to put a bullet in-between Michael Myers’ eyes. Will he succeed?? Is he a bigger danger to Haddonfield than Meyers himself?!

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
